stories.title: White Sox 8, Cubs 3 — Taillon's Five-HR Nightmare, Series Tied 1-1

x_posts.label: Post 1
x_posts.text:
White Sox 8, Cubs 3. Series tied 1-1.

Murakami went YARD twice. Five White Sox HRs off Taillon in 5 innings. Davis Martin: 6 IP, 1 ER, 7 Ks.

PCA and Amaya homered. Not enough.

Rubber game today, 1:10 PM CT.

#Cubs #GoCubs #FlyTheW

research.facts:
White Sox 8, Cubs 3 — Taillon's Five-HR Nightmare
- **[HIGH]** Final score: White Sox 8, Cubs 3. (CBS News Chicago, ESPN, Fox Sports, multiple sources.)
- **[HIGH]** WP: Davis Martin (6-1). LP: Jameson Taillon (2-3). (ESPN box score, Cubs Insider recap.)
- **[HIGH]** Taillon surrendered career-high five home runs in 5-plus innings. (MLB.com, Cubs Insider, Chicago Sun-Times — all confirm "career-high.") Consistent across 4+ sources.
- **[HIGH]** Murakami hit two home runs — first career multi-HR game. HR #17 was 428 ft to center in 5th inning. 3 RBIs. (MLB.com, ESPN, Sun-Times.)
- **[HIGH]** Vargas, Montgomery, Benintendi also homered. (Fox 32 Chicago, ESPN.)
- **[HIGH]** Davis Martin: 6 IP, 1 ER, 7 Ks. (ESPN box score, multiple sources.)
- **[HIGH]** Pete Crow-Armstrong and Miguel Amaya homered for Cubs. (Initial search summary; consistent with recap headlines.)
- **[MEDIUM]** White Sox improve to 6-2 on nine-game homestand. (Single summary source — plausible given series result.)
- **[MEDIUM]** Cubs record going into today: ~29-17 (29-16 after Game 1 per 5/16 story history, -1 for Game 2 loss).
research.sources: Sources: CBS News Chicago, ESPN, Cubs Insider, Fox 32 Chicago, Fox Sports, Chicago Sun-Times, MLB.com.

stories.title: Five Home Runs in Five Innings — The Rotation Can't Keep This Up

x_posts.label: Post 2
x_posts.text:
Five home runs. Career-high. In 5 innings.

Taillon quietly held this rotation together. Last night, it came apart.

Horton's gone for the year. Steele's timeline slipping. Boyd's on the mend. The Cubs need a starter — NOW.

#Cubs #MLB #CubsBaseball

research.facts:
Five Home Runs in Five Innings — The Rotation Can't Keep This Up
- **[HIGH]** Taillon allowed career-high 5 HRs in Game 2. (Multiple sources — see Story 1.)
- **[HIGH]** Cade Horton: 60-day IL, second Tommy John surgery (UCL revision repair April 16), out ~15-16 months, effectively season-ending. (MLB Trade Rumors, ESPN, multiple sources.)
- **[HIGH]** Justin Steele: UCL revision repair, setback reported, likely beyond All-Star break (worse than original timeline). (Fansided, search results.)
- **[HIGH]** Matthew Boyd: meniscus surgery May 6, expected return late June. (MLB Trade Rumors, ESPN, CBS Sports, multiple sources.)
- **[MEDIUM]** Taillon record now 2-3. (Derived from "2-3" in game result source — plausible.)
research.sources: Sources: MLB Trade Rumors, ESPN, Fansided, CBS Sports, Cubs Insider.

stories.title: Boyd Threw Off the Mound Saturday — Help Is Coming

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
Matthew Boyd threw off the mound Saturday.

After meniscus surgery on May 6, he's targeting a late June return — a bullpen session is next.

Rotation help is on the way. Can't come soon enough. 💪

#Cubs #CubsBaseball #MLB

research.facts:
Boyd Threw Off the Mound Saturday
- **[HIGH]** Matthew Boyd threw off the mound Saturday May 16. (CBS Sports: "Scheduled to throw off mound on Saturday, May 16." SI.com confirms positive timeline update.)
- **[HIGH]** Surgery was May 6 meniscus repair. (MLB Trade Rumors: "May 6." ESPN: "at least one month after knee surgery.")
- **[HIGH]** Return target: late June. ("Week of June 22 potentially marking his return" — ClutchPoints, FanSided. "Late June or early July" — ESPN.)
- **[HIGH]** Bullpen session expected in following week after mound session. (CBS Sports.)
- **[MEDIUM]** He tore the meniscus "sitting down to play with his kids" — colorful detail from Yahoo Sports headline. Single source, not used in tweet but noted.
research.sources: Sources: CBS Sports, MLB Trade Rumors, ESPN, SI.com, ClutchPoints, FanSided.

stories.title: NL Central Pulse — Cubs Lead, Cardinals Lurking, Brewers Rolling

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
Cubs lead the NL Central — but the division is tightening.

Cardinals within striking distance. Brewers riding a four-game win streak with Misiorowski emerging.

The rotation is a real risk. Every series counts now.

#Cubs #NorthSiders #MLB

research.facts:
NL Central Pulse
- **[MEDIUM]** Cardinals record ~25-18 as of 5/14-5/16 (from 5/16 story history and search results; no fresh specific number confirmed today).
- **[MEDIUM]** Brewers four-game win streak (from 5/16 story history covering Misiorowski's 6-IP shutout vs Yankees).
- **[MEDIUM]** Jacob Misiorowski (Brewers) threw 6 shutout innings, 11 Ks, in that win. (5/16 story history, single source.)
- **[MEDIUM]** NL Central all five teams above .500 through early May. (MSN article referenced in search.)
- **[LOW]** Cubs ~29-17 entering today (derived; not confirmed from a live standings source).
Note: NL Central standings sites (ESPN, BB-Ref, CBS) all returned 403 today. Using 5/16 pipeline context and game results to estimate. Do not use specific "games behind" numbers in tweet — keep to qualitative language ("within striking distance").
research.sources: Sources: 5/16 story history context, Brew Crew Ball Power Rankings (Week 7 reference), MSN NL Central article.

stories.title: Series Decider — Rea vs Fedde, 1:10 PM CT

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
Series decider. Right now.

Colin Rea (4-2) vs Erick Fedde (0-4, 3.77 ERA) — don't let the record fool you. Fedde has been sharp.

Rate Field. 1:10 PM CT. Let's take the Crosstown.

#Cubs #GoCubs #FlyTheW

research.facts:
Series Decider — Rea vs Fedde
- **[HIGH]** Game 3 at Guaranteed Rate Field, 1:10 PM CT. (Multiple betting/preview sites, ESPN gameday link, CBS Sports schedule — 2:10 ET = 1:10 CT confirmed.)
- **[HIGH]** Colin Rea record: 4-2, 4.68 ERA. (FanDuel Research, Bleacher Nation preview from May 13.)
- **[HIGH]** Erick Fedde record: 0-4, 3.77 ERA. (Multiple preview sources.)
- **[HIGH]** Series tied 1-1 (Cubs won Game 1 10-5; White Sox won Game 2 8-3).
- **[MEDIUM]** White Sox record 22-21 entering series. (UrbanMatter article, single source.)
- **[HIGH]** Fedde ERA vs record dichotomy (0-4 despite 3.77 ERA) confirmed — multiple previews cite this, noting White Sox run support issues.
research.sources: Sources: ESPN, Bleacher Nation, FanDuel Research, CBS Sports, Cubs Insider (May 15 preview).

x_posts.label: Post 6
x_posts.text:
The Mets have a June 1 decision point on Freddy Peralta (3.12 ERA, pending FA).

Counsell managed him in Milwaukee for years. Cubs need arms. Taillon just gave up FIVE home runs.

The market won't wait.

#Cubs #MLB #CubsBaseball

research.facts:
The Peralta Clock Is Ticking
- **[HIGH]** Mets set June 1 as decision point for Peralta — get back in race or move him. (MLB Trade Rumors: "June 1st is a target time for the Mets to either get back into the race or move the soon to be free agent Peralta.")
- **[HIGH]** Peralta's 2026 ERA: 3.12. (MLB Trade Rumors: "entering Friday's action with a 3.12 ERA.")
- **[HIGH]** Peralta is a pending free agent. (Multiple sources — turns 30 next month.)
- **[HIGH]** Peralta was acquired from Brewers by Mets in offseason (Sproat + Jett Williams to Milwaukee). (MLB Trade Rumors, reviewing the brew.)
- **[HIGH]** Craig Counsell managed Peralta in Milwaukee. (Multiple sources confirm; tenure 2015-2023 Brewers; Peralta debuted with Brewers 2017.)
- **[MEDIUM]** Cubs "made calls on pitching" — Cubs source told reporters they've spoken with many teams about pitching, not Peralta specifically with Mets yet. (Heavy.com.)
- **[HIGH]** Cubs confirmed exploring early market for SP. (MLB Trade Rumors headline: "Cubs Exploring Early Market For Starting Pitching.")
research.sources:
Sources: MLB Trade Rumors, Yahoo Sports, Heavy.com, SI.com, Reviewing the Brew.
## Cross-Check: Compound Claims
Per the two-source rule, the following compound claims were verified against 2+ sources:
| Claim | Sources | Status |
|-------|---------|--------|
| Taillon career-high 5 HRs | MLB.com, Cubs Insider, Sun-Times, Fox 32 | HIGH ✅ |
| Murakami first career multi-HR game | MLB.com, ESPN | HIGH ✅ |
| Davis Martin 6 IP, 1 ER, 7 Ks | ESPN box score, CBS Sports | HIGH ✅ |
| Boyd threw off mound May 16 | CBS Sports, confirmed by SI.com update | HIGH ✅ |
| Game 3: 1:10 PM CT, Rea vs Fedde | ESPN, FanDuel, CBS Sports | HIGH ✅ |
| Peralta 3.12 ERA, pending FA | MLB Trade Rumors x2, Yahoo Sports | HIGH ✅ |
| Mets June 1 Peralta decision | MLB Trade Rumors | MEDIUM — single outlet |
